title: Anyone can trigger the one-shot bounty sweep and consume opportunities at arbitrary times
reason: `executeOnOpportunity()` is permissionless, but permissionless execution alone is not a reportable flaw from this code. The claimed harm depends on unproven assumptions about privileged operator intent or one-shot target-side semantics; the concrete guard-bypass angle is captured separately in F-007.

title: `endPool` is attempted even when `startPool` failed, expanding the attack surface to pre-existing pools
reason: The code does unconditionally call `endPool`, but reportable harm depends entirely on undocumented `SILICA.endPool` behavior for pre-existing pools. Without evidence that `endPool` can damage unrelated pools in this situation, the candidate remains too speculative.
